arcadia_guard: Dr. T...? Hold it right there. Who are you and where did you hear that name? You'd better start talking, or my Zoid will do the asking.
bianco_interrogation: Alright, alright! I'll talk! Just... keep that dinosaur away from me. We were hired to find a girl. We thought it could be that kid, so we took her to our boss.
...you don't want to mess with him. That's all I know, I swear!
bianco: Hey, have you heard? There's some kid wandering around the ruins again. Apparently he plays soldier out in the desert, pretending to be some kind of warrior like his old man.

Problem is, he keeps stumbling into our operations. Blu tried to chase him off with his Guysack, but the brat won't take a hint. Let's head to the ruins and help Blu get rid of that nuisance once and for all.
becker_scan: Oh, a customer! People rarely buy Core Preservers from me, so let me give you a tip to get the most out of them.

When you're fighting a Zoid Sleeper, activate the preserver before the battle ends. If you weaken it enough, the preserver will try to extract and preserve its Core. Planning to fight several in a row? Double-click and the preserver will activate automatically in every battle.

Just keep in mind, each preserver must be sterile to avoid damaging the Core, so they can only be used once. You'll need one per extraction. But hey, no complaints from me... the more you buy, the better for business!
becker_scan_gift: Welcome! Jenkins told me you'd be stopping by. Let me explain how Core Preservers work.

When you're fighting a Zoid Sleeper, activate the preserver before the battle ends. If you weaken it enough, the preserver will try to extract and preserve its Core. Planning to fight several in a row? Double-click and the preserver will activate automatically in every battle.

Just keep in mind, each preserver must be sterile to avoid damaging the Core, so they can only be used once. You'll need one per extraction. But hey, no complaints from me... the more you buy, the better for business!
Here, take these 5 Core Preservers on the house. You'll need them to extract the Cores of those Sleepers out there. Good luck!
boy: Please, you have to help me! A bandit came and took my sister and my mother! They went toward the abandoned camp to the north... I'm so scared for them.
But be careful, the bandit is really strong! You should take the intermediate route to get there. You'll encounter Zoids Sleepers along the way, it's good practice for your combat techniques.
captain: HEEEEEEY!! I need your help. Bandits have been raiding our village. They grow bolder each day.
All our soldiers were recruited for the war between the Republic and the Empire. There is no one left to defend us. I'm too old to fight anymore. My body can't take the strain of piloting a Zoid.
But I still have three Zoids from my days as a soldier. They're outdated... but they always saved my life. Would you pilot one of them to protect this village?
captain_debrief: Thank you for your help, I knew there was something special about you... Hmmm an artifact used by the bandits? Let me take a look...

Ohh back in my days in the army I saw these kinds of artifacts, but this one looks far more advanced than any I knew... If I'm not mistaken, it's a Sleeper module. Some people in the army only care about the number of Zoids available for battle.

So they invented the Sleeper system as a way for pilots to control multiple Zoids at the same time. It seems these inventions have fallen into the wrong hands, that's why there are so many Zoids Sleepers roaming around here... I'm almost certain the bandits brought them.
But make no mistake, young pilot, not all army inventions are bad... If you earn its trust, this Sleeper system would allow you to bond with several Zoids at the same time.

If you really plan to face those bandits, you could use some reinforcements. For that you'll need more Zoids. Why don't you talk to old Jenkins? He used to work in a Zoid laboratory, maybe he can help you.
captain_pride: I've been hearing stories about you, kid. Helping villagers, taking on bandits, protecting people you don't even know... You've become someone this region can count on.
You know, when I gave you that old Zoid of mine, I wasn't sure what to expect. But seeing what you've done with it... it fills this old soldier with pride.
These are dangerous times. The war between the Republic and the Empire is far from over, and people like you tend to attract attention from both sides. Watch your back out there.
captain_farewell: Well, well! Now you actually look like a proper army commander. I can see you're determined to take on those bandits. Good luck, kid.

Oh, one more thing. I took a closer look at that Sleeper module of yours. I wouldn't recommend deploying more than one Zoid of the same species. The module doesn't seem to allow live control of multiple Zoids of the same kind.

So if you're building your army, variety will be your best weapon... For now. Be careful out there. In these times of war, it's best not to get mixed up in military affairs. But something tells me it's too late for that...
challenge: But choosing a Zoid isn't enough. The Zoid must choose you too. Prove your worth. Climb up to the cockpit and show it you have the spirit of a pilot!
explorer_dungeon_tips: You're heading into the ruins? I've been down there a few times myself. Let me tell you what to expect.
If you manage to weaken a Zoid enough to preserve its Core, take the chance to collect some Zi metal too. You'll need it to prepare your Zoids for sorties into the ruins.

Once inside, you'll run into hostile Sleepers near the entrance. But the deeper you go, the more dangerous they become. Some are far more dangerous than the rest, but they usually guard more valuable treasures.

The ruins are full of surprises. Strange things happen down there... you'll have to make choices that could help you or make things worse. And if you're lucky, you might stumble upon some supplies along the way.
Whatever you're looking for, don't go in unless you're ready for a real fight.
fisherman_docks: Heading out to sea? Watch yourselves. There have been Zoid attacks on ships lately. The waters around here are crawling with wild aquatic Zoids.

Remember, not every Zoid can handle the water. A land-based Zoid won't be able to fight at sea, so don't count on its firepower out there. An air Zoid might hold its own, but its attack power will drop to about a quarter of what it normally packs. You'll be sitting ducks.
Make sure you've got aquatic Zoids in your ranks before you set sail. Trust me, you don't want to learn that lesson the hard way.
farewell: Ha! I knew you had it in you. The Zoid has accepted you as its pilot.
Now go and help our neighbors. The bandits won't wait forever. Be careful out there, kid. And take good care of that Zoid.
fiona_oasis: Hello... I'm Fine. Van told me you helped him at the ruins. Thank you! He's a good person, and this place is so peaceful...
You found something at the ruins? Let me see...
Ughh, my head!... I'm sorry, I don't know what happened. That name just came to me. I don't remember what it means, but it feels important.
fiona_rescue: Thank you so much for your help. I'm sorry I can't tell you anything useful about the statue, but I know you're not just good at fighting — you can listen to your Zoids.
Never forget: they are living beings too.
girl_thanks: Thank you for saving me! I was so scared...
They kept asking me about my father, but I didn't tell them anything. He told me his work is very important and must be kept secret from bad people.
I don't know where he is right now, but I know he's doing something important. Thank you again for rescuing me. Could you take me back to the village?
kara_care_creature: It resembles an Organoid — like Zeke. But I ran the same tests I would on others and it does not seem to share the same internal structures. Its behavior does not match either — it does not respond to stimuli, does not interact. Whatever this creature is, it is something else entirely.

It has been at the sanctuary for a while. It ignores the other Zoids completely — does not interact, does not follow them. The only thing it seems to want to do is feed on metals. Some of the Zoids even attacked it and it did not even try to defend itself.

Thank you for looking after it, Miss Unia. I don't care what it is or what it can't do. Have you already forgotten it saved our lives against Rosso? And that it stopped the Stray Helcat in the ruins? I think Beacon is amazing. It just woke up in a world that is nothing like the one it knew — of course it is confused. Give it time.
You might be right, little one. By the way, I heard your father returned to Porto Nido yesterday. Why don't you go see him? I'm sure he'd love to meet Beacon.
jenkins: So Malinoff sent you? Well, well... I saw how you helped the people of this village, so I'll hear you out. More Zoids, you say? The easiest way is buying them from the army, though they'll only sell you their scraps.

I stopped working with Zoids a long time ago, but back in my day we used traps to catch wild Zoids. Nowadays it's nearly impossible to find them, let alone get one to cooperate with you. So the armies came up with a new method: we learned to incubate Zoid Cores.

As you know, the Zoid Core is the vital nucleus of every Zoid. Each species has a different Core, but they all share the same origin: the metals dissolved in the planet's primordial waters. What we managed to do was recreate the conditions of those ancient waters in a laboratory. If you gather enough Zoid Magnite, or failing that, plenty of Magnis, we can submerge it in that substance and create a brand new Zoid Core.

The next step is shaping that Core into the species you need. For that, you'll need the Zoid's structural data. Here, take this. It's a Core Analyzer. With it, you can analyze the data from a preserved Zoid Core and get the blueprint we need to shape a new one after that species.

There are a lot of Zoid Sleepers roaming this region lately. If you come across one, try to weaken it and preserve its Core before it's destroyed. Fair warning though, no Zoid will just let you access its Core. You'll have to immobilize it first. I won't lie to you, taking down a Zoid without destroying it is no easy feat.

Once you've got the data from a Zoid's core, come see me and we can clone it in the lab. With this basic lab, we can only hatch simple Zoids, and I can't promise a high success rate. One last thing: to extract and preserve the Cores you'll need a Core Preserver. They're single-use, so you'll have to stock up. Visit Mrs. Becker's workshop, she should have them.
jenkins_to_work: Well, well! So you actually managed to get a Zoid's structural data. Not bad for a rookie. Alright, time to get to work. Let's head to my humble lab and see what we can create with what you've got.
And don't worry about the cost for the first one. Consider it a gift for your help to the village.
maria_fiona: You were worried about Van? He left early with that girl and that dinosaur they found. I think he's showing her around the town. That boy... he's always getting himself into these situations.
But a girl with no memories? That's... concerning. If you see him, please tell him to be careful. And... thank you for your help.
maria_flyheight: My brother Van? *sighs* He's out there again, isn't he? He's always dreamed of being a great warrior like our father. He spends his days exploring the Elmia Desert, pretending he's on some grand adventure.
He's probably out there right now, wandering around the ruins. If you're heading that way, please watch out for him. He's reckless, but he means well.
The desert is east of here. Be careful, the Sleepers out there are stronger than the ones near the village.
maria_van_gone: So... Van really left, didn't he? I had a feeling he would. He's just like our father... He needs to keep moving. Thank you for everything. Knowing that you tried... it means a lot. Take care of yourself out there, okay?
maria_van_fled: Thanks for your help! Van arrived with Zeke and the Shield Liger and took out those bandits. It was incredible!
But Van... he said he's putting everyone in danger by staying here. That he's leaving to protect us.

He's always wanted to be like our father and take care of everyone, but he's still my little brother. Please, you have to stop him! If you hurry, you might catch him before he disappears into the desert.
narration_arcobaleno_plan: You hide among the camp tents and overhear Rosso talking to his men.
"With the silver Zoid and the girl, we'll finally have what we need to return to the Imperial Army. High command will welcome us back as heroes."
"Bianco, Nero, Blu... head to the exchange point and make sure everything goes according to plan. If those villagers try anything, you know what to do."
narration_beacon_emerges: The Ancient Tortoise raises its cannon, ready to fire again. But then it stops. Its eyes lock onto Kara — or rather, onto what she carries.

Kara steps forward, trembling, holding the ancient statue against her chest. The Ancient Tortoise lowers its cannon slowly and lets out a deep, mournful sound — like a call it has not made in centuries.

The statue answers. A pulse of light erupts from within, then another, stronger, faster. The stone-like crust that covered the statue begins to shift — pieces rearranging themselves, stretching into thin metallic threads that wrap around the Core and begin to give it shape.
The threads finish weaving. Something small and strange stands where the statue once was. It moves — but it is unlike anything they have ever seen.
Is that... a Zoid? It is so small. It does not look like any Zoid I have ever seen.

The creature turns toward the Ancient Tortoise. For a moment, neither moves. Then the creature lets out a faint sound — and the Cannon Tortoise answers with a low, gentle rumble. Like old friends meeting after a long sleep.
The Ancient Tortoise, exhausted from the battle, lowers itself into the sand. Its eyes close slowly as the desert swallows it — not fleeing, but resting. Returning to a sleep it has earned.
The creature watches it go. Then it stands still, trembling, unsure of anything around it.
You told me Fine said a name when she saw the statue... Beacon, right?
The creature's ears twitch. It turns toward Kara.
It is okay, Beacon. You are scared, aren't you? It is okay. I am here.
narration_bandits_flee: Rosso's Red Horn staggers back, heavily damaged. You seize the moment to recover the girl. Rosso glares at you with fury, but then explosions at the exchange point catch his attention. Something didn't go according to plan.
"This isn't over," he growls. "We'll find what we're looking for, with or without the girl." The remaining bandits retreat into the desert, disappearing in a cloud of dust.
The Arcobaleno Camp falls silent. The girl is safe.
narration_colony_attack: As you approach Wind Colony, you hear explosions and the unmistakable roar of Zoid engines.
More bandits are attacking the colony. They demand Zeke be handed over and are threatening the townspeople. They've taken Maria hostage!
Van and his Shield Liger are too far away to help. It's up to you to repel the attackers!
narration_discover_fione: After the miracle you just witnessed, you return to the ruins searching for any sign of Van. Deep within the crumbling passages, you hear he's in a chamber similar to where they found Zeke.
Suddenly, another capsule opens and a girl emerges from it. But at that very moment, weakened by the battle, the ground beneath you gives way and you fall into a hidden chamber.
In the center stands a small ancient statue, its surface covered in strange symbols.
narration_maria_kidnapped: The Arcobaleno gang flees the colony upon discovering it's not unprotected. But in the chaos, they managed to take Maria with them.
"Hand over the silver Zoid if you ever want to see her again!" the bandits shout as they retreat.
narration_officer_deal_imperial: The Republican Officer's Barigator collapses onto the shore. He stumbles out of the cockpit, drenched and furious.
Defeated... by Imperial scum?! This is an act of war! I will report this to Central Command and they will send an entire battalion!
You will do no such thing. Sit down and listen.
Do you realize what you have done, Corin?! You let an Imperial soldier fight on your behalf! This is collaboration with the enemy! They will brand you a traitor!

This island is neutral ground. It has been for decades. No faction has jurisdiction here. The only traitor here is someone who would threaten endangered Zoids to further his career. And you accepted this duel willingly, in front of witnesses. A report would ruin you, not me.
So here is what happens. You leave this island and forget what you saw. We will not report your defeat, and I will contact President Camford directly. That should satisfy your orders well enough.
...Enjoy this while it lasts, Imperial scum. Next time, there will be no duel. No deal. Just the full might of the Republic.
The officer climbs back into his battered Barigator and retreats across the water. As the sound of its engines fades into the distance, the island falls silent once more.
narration_officer_deal_neutral: The Republican Officer's Barigator collapses onto the shore. He stumbles out of the cockpit, drenched and furious.
Impossible...! I was given direct orders from President Camford herself! I will NOT return empty-handed! I am calling reinforcements!
And what will you tell them? That you lost a duel you accepted willingly? That you caused a stampede in a protected sanctuary? I doubt the President would be pleased to hear any of that.
You think you can just make this go away?! I have a duty to fulfill!
Then listen carefully. You leave this island and forget everything you saw here. In return, we will not report this incident to your superiors. No defeat on your record, no disciplinary hearing.
You expect me to abandon my mission and pretend none of this happened?!
Your mission was to bring me before the President. I will contact President Camford directly, on my own terms. That should satisfy your orders well enough.
...Fine. But mark my words, this is not over. The Republic does not forget.
The officer climbs back into his battered Barigator and retreats across the water. As the sound of its engines fades into the distance, the island falls silent once more.
narration_officer_deal_republican: The Republican Officer's Barigator collapses onto the shore. He stumbles out of the cockpit, drenched and furious.
Defeated... by one of our own?! This is treason! You attacked a superior officer carrying out a direct order from President Camford! I will have you court-martialed!
Enough. No one is getting court-martialed today.
This soldier defied a direct order and humiliated the Republic! I will make sure Command hears every detail!
And I will make sure they hear how you terrorized a protected sanctuary, provoked a stampede, and then lost a duel you accepted of your own free will. How do you think that report will end for you?

Here is what is going to happen. You leave this island and forget everything you saw. We will not mention your defeat, and you will not mention any of this. I will contact President Camford myself. That should satisfy your orders well enough.
...You will regret this. All of you. The Republic does not forget, and neither will I.
The officer climbs back into his battered Barigator and retreats across the water. As the sound of its engines fades into the distance, the island falls silent once more.
narration_tortoise_appears: The last Cannon Tortoise retreats into the sand. The desert falls silent — too silent. Then, from atop a weathered dune near the Arcobaleno Camp, a massive silhouette rises. A Cannon Tortoise unlike any other — its shell scarred by centuries, its cannon worn by battles long forgotten.
That's the one! I remember it — the same markings, the same scars. It saved us from Rosso's Red Horn!
It looks angry... really angry. But we need it — if we have to fight it to get close, then we fight it!
narration_turtle_savior: Rosso's Red Horn is like a walking fortress. Your Zoids can barely withstand the relentless attacks...
Your Zoid is about to fall when the ancient statue in your pack pulses with a strange light.
Rosso lines up his Red Horn for a final shot. You dodge by a hair, but he charges in for the kill.
Suddenly, a blast from the distance strikes Rosso's Red Horn squarely in the side, with enough force to knock it down.
Far away, among the desert dunes, you can see a Cannon Tortoise, ancient and weathered, sinking back into the sand.
The blast has shattered the Red Horn's armor and created a weak spot. This is your chance!
narration_van_farewell: During the battle, you understood why Zeke is special. His power to fuse with other Zoids and restore them is something you've never seen before.
A power like that will attract many who won't stop until they claim it for themselves. As long as Zeke exists, Van will never be safe in one place.
You decide to step aside. You watch them walk away together toward the horizon: Van, Zeke, and Fine, ready to face whatever comes next.
narration_republican_intervention: The Zoids in the sanctuary grow restless. Their movements become erratic, agitated by something approaching from beyond the trees.
Something is wrong. The Zoids can sense it... they are frightened. Did you bring more people with you?!
No one followed us. This is not our doing.
narration_shield_liger: In the middle of the battle, Van bursts out of the ruins, carried by Zeke. Bianco and Nero turn around and chase him. Your Zoids are still regrouping and you can't reach Van in time. He's cornered.

But then something strange happens: Zeke fuses with one of the destroyed Zoids from the ruins, and suddenly it rises, fully restored. And in a matter of seconds, Van and the Shield Liger drive the bandits away.
planet_intro: On Planet Zi, beyond the Milky Way, there exist steel Zoids with outstanding combat capability. The Zoids carry within them a warrior spirit.

They are the most powerful weapon in the war between the Empire and the Republic of Planet Zi. For decades, this war has ravaged these lands. Amidst this great conflict, smaller villages struggle to survive.
In one of these forgotten villages, a new threat approaches...
priest_leon: Hmm, a missing girl, you say? I'm afraid we haven't heard anything about that here in Wind Colony. But... if anyone would know about the bandits' movements, it's that boy who's always getting himself into trouble with them.
I don't know what drives him, but he's constantly venturing into the desert to confront them. Quite reckless if you ask me. You should speak with his sister. She might know where to find him.
priest_leon_exchange: I'm sorry Van, but Maria comes first. Hand over Zeke! If you resist, we'll have to tie you up.
The patriarch and I will go make the exchange.
republican_officer_imperial: Now hold on. You cannot just barge in here and threaten people.
Surely there is a more civilized way to handle this.
Stay out of this. This is official Republic business.
Wait... Imperial insignia?! What is the Guylos Empire doing on this island? So that is why you refuse to cooperate, Corin. You have already sided with the enemy.
That is absurd! I have not sided with anyone!
Save your excuses. I will deal with all of you at once!
Enough! I will not tolerate fighting on this island! The Zoids have suffered enough already!
*sniff*... The Zoids... they are so scared... Please, can we help them? We have to do something!
Wait. Officer, there is no need for violence. Miss Corin will go with you.
What?! Thrun, what are you doing?!
Trust me. We need to get them away from the sanctuary before the Zoids get hurt.
Once they reach the coast, far from here... that will be your moment to act.
Finally, someone with sense. Move out.
republican_officer_neutral: Now hold on. You cannot just barge in here and threaten people.
Surely there is a more civilized way to handle this.
Stay out of this. This is official Republic business.
And who are the rest of you? What are you doing here? This is an official matter of the Helic Republic. No unauthorized personnel should be present.
I do not know who you are or what you want with Corin, but I will not tolerate any more interference. Step aside or face the consequences!
Enough! I will not tolerate fighting on this island! The Zoids have suffered enough already!
*sniff*... The Zoids... they are so scared... Please, can we help them? We have to do something!
Wait. Officer, there is no need for violence. Miss Corin will go with you.
What?! Thrun, what are you doing?!
Trust me. We need to get them away from the sanctuary before the Zoids get hurt.
Once they reach the coast, far from here... that will be your moment to act.
Finally, someone with sense. Move out.
republican_officer_republican: Now hold on. You cannot just barge in here and threaten people.
Surely there is a more civilized way to handle this.
Stay out of this. This is official Republic business.
Wait... I know you. You serve the Republic. Were you sent to complete this mission before me? Trying to take the credit?
It does not matter. I was given this order, and I will carry it out. Stand aside, or I will consider this insubordination!
Enough! I will not tolerate fighting on this island! The Zoids have suffered enough already!
*sniff*... The Zoids... they are so scared... Please, can we help them? We have to do something!
Wait. Officer, there is no need for violence. Miss Corin will go with you.
What?! Thrun, what are you doing?!
Trust me. We need to get them away from the sanctuary before the Zoids get hurt.
Once they reach the coast, far from here... that will be your moment to act.
Finally, someone with sense. Move out.
rosso_confrontation: Well, well... looks like we've got a rat. So you're the one who took the Sleeper module I gave that good-for-nothing.

Incredible technology, isn't it? A contact provided it to us in exchange for a job. We need to deliver this girl's father. His research is apparently worth more than you can imagine. So she's just the bait.

But you... you shouldn't have stuck your nose where it doesn't belong. You may have many Zoids under your command, but none of them are a match for a true warrior like me. My Red Horn will tear you apart!
scrap_dealer: Galos, Zi-Dollars, Zenys... all those currencies, one day they're worth a fortune, and the next they won't buy you a grain of salt. You want some advice, kid? Keep your wealth in materials.
Me? I collect Magnis, tiny shards of Zoid Magnite you can salvage from destroyed Zoids. They're usually concentrated around the pipes connected to the Zoid Core.
Next time you take one down, don't forget to check the wreckage. You'd be surprised what you can find.
unia_officer_confrontation: What do you think you are doing?! You are scaring the Zoids! This is a protected sanctuary!
Unia Corin. By order of the Helic Republic Army, you are to report to Central Command immediately.
I already told your superiors. I have no interest in rejoining the military. I have work to do here.
These are direct orders from President Camford herself.
The President...? I... Despite everything, I still have the deepest respect for that woman. But she knows better than anyone that I will not take part in this war.
I have my orders, and I will not return empty-handed. You will come with us, one way or another.

Stop! Do not take another step! Can you not see what you are doing?! The Zoids are panicking! If you keep pushing forward you will cause a stampede! These creatures are under my protection and I will NOT let you harm them!
unia_show_dragon_horse: Miss Unia, Miss Unia! Come look! The Core — it worked! Little pieces started growing out of it and they kept forming and forming until... look! It's a Zoid!

A Dragon Horse. It has been a long time since I've seen one. They were mounts in the old world, before the armies built cockpits and needed bigger Zoids to pilot. Too small for war, too free-spirited to control. The armies discarded them, and the wars destroyed their habitats. They are nearly extinct now.
And yet here one is. Not stamped out of a cloning lab — this is how a Zoid should come into the world. You must take good care of it. A species this rare deserves better than what the world gave it.

Good. Now — we have waited long enough. The statue. The mummy of a Core I told you about. We have seen it pulse twice. Whatever is inside, it is alive, and this method can make Zoids emerge from a Core.
I do not know if it will work with something this ancient, but we must try.
unia_statue_wont_emerge: The statue has absorbed every fragment we have given it. It seems fully nurtured — but it will not respond. No growth, no formation, nothing. I was afraid of this. Whatever is inside, it is far older than any Core my family has documented.

I went through my grandmother's notes again last night and found a passage about something she called 'resonance bonds.' Some ancient Cores could not develop alone — they needed the presence of a bonded Zoid, a living creature tied to the Core through shared experience. Without that presence, the Core refuses to awaken.
A bonded Zoid...? Wait! The turtle! In the desert, when we fought Rosso, the statue glowed and a Cannon Tortoise appeared out of nowhere and saved us!
The statue reacted to a Cannon Tortoise? And it appeared right when the statue pulsed? That could be exactly the kind of resonance bond my grandmother described.

But that was months ago, in the middle of the Elmia Desert. We do not even know if the creature is still alive. Dr. T and your father know more about Zoid behavior in the wild than any of us — perhaps they know something.
unia_corin_meeting: This is a restricted area! Who authorized your presence here? State your business immediately.
It has been a long time, Miss Corin. Forgive the sudden visit.
Dr. Thrun?! I... I was not expecting you. You could have sent word ahead. Forgive my reaction, please come in. Any acquaintance of the doctor is welcome here.

Allow me to introduce her properly. This is Unia Corin. An extraordinary pilot who served in the Helic Republic. She left the military to dedicate herself to something far more important: breeding and conserving Zoid species.

Welcome. That is right, my family has been breeding Zoids for generations. I served as a pilot for the Helic Republic, but my mission is greater than that. Zoids should never be reduced to mere tools of war. This sanctuary ensures their future.
Miss Corin, we have come a long way. We found something in the ruins that we cannot understand. Dr. Thrun said you might be able to help us. Would you take a look?

Hmm... Let me see... Interesting. From certain angles this could resemble a Zoid Core, but it is ancient beyond measure. I would call it the mummy of a Core. I cannot believe this! I have spent my entire life studying Cores and I have never seen anything like it! Where on Zi did you find this?
We found it in the Elmia Ruins. A couple of times it has emitted energy pulses that saved us in desperate situations.

Wait... Energy pulses? I thought this was a mummy, a fossil... but if it has emitted pulses then it is alive. A Core this ancient, alive... Now I understand why you came to see me. You want to awaken it.

First of all, I do not even know if I could. I have never worked with anything like this. If it were up to me... I would love to keep it. I would give anything to study a Core like this and try to revive it. But I know you will not leave it with me. So take it with you, but I cannot help you awaken it. Not during wartime. My family has accumulated knowledge about Cores over generations, and sharing it now could give any side a devastating advantage.

I am sorry, truly. I wish I could do more. But if that Core has survived entire eras waiting in silence... perhaps that is how it should remain. Now I must ask you to leave. The Zoids in this sanctuary are not accustomed to human presence, and the longer you stay, the more restless they will become. When this war is over, if you still need me, you know where to find me.
unia_corin_summary: I already told you, I cannot help you awaken that Core. Not while this war continues. My family has guarded these secrets for generations and I will not be the one to break that oath.
What I can tell you is that what you carry is no ordinary relic. If it has reacted on its own, it means it still has a will. Treat it with respect. When the war ends, come back and we will talk.
sanctuary_secrets: I promised to share my family's knowledge with you, and I will. But knowledge without experience is just words. So we are going to do this the other way around. Hold this. This is a living Zoid Core. Small, dormant, but alive. Every Zoid you have ever seen began as something like this.
It is... warm. I can feel it humming, like a tiny heartbeat.
Good. That means it is healthy. Your first duty as guardians of this sanctuary: bring this Core to life. Nurture it until it grows into a Zoid.
Nurture it...? How do we do that?

That is what I am about to teach you. In ancient times, Zoid Cores were born in the primordial seas of Planet Zi, waters rich in dissolved metal ions found nowhere else. As a Core absorbed those metals, it grew. Pits formed on its surface, and from those pits, skeletal parts began to sprout. The number and shape of those parts determined the species.

Once their skeletons were sturdy enough, some climbed onto land. There they fed on metal-rich soil, ore, even scrap, and grew steadily over decades. Each generation stronger, better adapted to its environment than the last. That is how the cycle continued for millions of years.

The primordial seas changed. The waters that gave birth to Zoids no longer exist in nature, and wild Cores became impossibly rare. When the armies discovered they could clone Cores in laboratories... why bother preserving what nature built?

Cloning keeps the war machine running, but it comes at a cost. A cloned Core is a copy, frozen in place. It will never grow beyond what it already is. No new traits, no adaptation, no improvement across generations. They are tools, not living creatures.

And when humans remodel them into Combat Zoids, adding cockpits, weapons, synthetic armor... the Core can never produce new Cores again. Every Combat Zoid on this planet is the last of its line. The armies have been sterilizing Zoids for generations without even realizing it. That is why I left the military. That is why this sanctuary exists.
That is horrible... All those Zoids out there fighting... none of them can ever...

Now you understand why this sanctuary exists. My family kept a secret for generations: the exact composition of the primordial waters. Every mineral, every trace element, in the precise balance that once filled the ancient seas. That knowledge is what makes us different from every laboratory on this planet.

Follow me. This is a nurturing tank. It replicates those primordial conditions. A Zoid born this way grows as nature intended. Not as a frozen copy, but as a living creature that adapts and strengthens with every cycle. And when it matures and produces new Cores, its offspring will carry everything their parent became, and surpass it. That is the true power of the natural cycle.

Maybe you have seen something like this in cartoons... feeding a Core with the energy of others. The difference is we do not destroy Zoids to do it. To nurture a Core, you need Core Fragments, pieces of living metal that concentrate wherever Zoids have fought. With the war raging, you will find plenty.

The sanctuary used to have a Gustav for mobile conservation work, but its Core died of old age last year. No replacement, no offspring. A Combat Zoid to the very end. What remains is the platform. Strap it to one of your Zoids to carry the tank while you travel. If you ever find a proper transport Zoid, it would work much better.
Now go. Collect fragments, feed that Core, and bring it to life. Show me what it means to be a guardian.
We will not let you down, Miss Corin. I promise.
sanctuary_secrets_summary: Remember, the nurturing tank replicates the primordial waters my family preserved for generations. Feed Core Fragments to your Cores and let nature do the rest. A Zoid born this way will grow stronger than anything the armies can clone.
unia_trials_accepted: I must admit... I did not think you would make it. Most would have given up after the first task. But here you are, with everything I asked for.
Perhaps you truly are worthy of my trust.
We could not have done it without you. Really, thank you.

However... there is a problem. The knowledge my family guards has never been shared with anyone outside this sanctuary. It is an oath that has been kept for generations, and I will not be the one to break it.

...Unless... if you were to become guardians of this sanctuary, sharing my knowledge with you would not break any oath. But let me be clear: becoming a guardian is a lifelong commitment. This oath stands above any army, any nation, any war. When I summon you, you will report. No questions, no delays. Think carefully before you accept.
We will. Whatever it takes, Miss Corin.

Then welcome to the Corin Sanctuary. From this day forward, you are one of us. You, little one, will be the Voice of the Sanctuary. And you, pilot, its Shield. As for you, doctors... do not take offense. I respect your research, Dr. Thrun, I truly do. But I know the mind of a scientist. The first thing you would do is apply these methods to your experiments, and that is exactly what this oath compels me to prevent.

I understand, Miss Corin. And you are right, I cannot promise that curiosity would not get the better of me. Your secret will be safer with them. We will return to Porto Nido, there is much to analyze with what we already know. Would you mind looking after Kara? I trust you, and in any case, I doubt she would agree to come back with me at this point.
unia_trials_intro: I owe you my thanks. Regardless of your allegiance, you put yourself on the line to protect the Zoids on this island. Not many would have done that. But gratitude is not the same as trust. I will not hand over generations of knowledge to a stranger.
Miss Corin... please. We came all this way because we need your help. Will you not at least give us a chance?

...When the officer barged into the sanctuary and the Zoids panicked, everyone was focused on the confrontation. Everyone except you. You were crying for them. You wanted to help them before anything else. That kind of instinct cannot be taught.

I still have my doubts about the rest of you. But if this girl is the one who wishes to awaken that Core, then perhaps I was wrong to refuse so quickly. I will give you a chance to earn my trust. Three tasks. Complete them, and we will talk.

The Barigator is one of the rarest species in these waters. I need structural data from five of them, obtained without harming them. That will prove you have the patience to study a Zoid, not just fight it.
Every conservation effort depends on proper Core handling. Bring me fifty Core Savers. That will prove you understand that preserving a Zoid requires care, resources, and dedication.

And finally... the Sea Panther. Nearly extinct because of this war. Both the Republic and the Empire would pay a fortune for one. If you acquire one and surrender it to me, giving up a Zoid that valuable... that will prove where your loyalties truly lie. Not with any army, but with the Zoids themselves.
Complete these three tasks and we will talk. I will be watching closely.
We will do it, Miss Corin. All of it. I promise you will not regret giving us this chance.
van_alert: Huh? Who are you? Wait... you're not one of those bandits, are you? My name's Van. Van Flyheight. I was hiding in here when one of those capsules opened and that Zoid came out and attacked me.
Bandits? More of them coming? I figured as much... So you can command Zoids? Great, then let's go after them and...
No time!! Maybe I can trick that Guysack guy and escape. Don't worry about me.
van_challenge: Ohh, it's you. Thanks for your help, Fine untied me and we managed to beat those scoundrels. I see you also found the girl you were looking for. Great!
However... I can't stay here. The bandits are after Zeke. If I stay, they'll surely come back.
What do you mean I can't leave on my own?! You think I'm not strong enough to protect us? Fine. I'll make you a deal. If you can beat me one-on-one, I'll stay.
But if I win, you let me go. No hard feelings. What do you say? Zeke and I against your best. Let's do this!
van_kidnapping: What?! They took Maria?! Those cowards...
This is my fault. I should have been there.
We need to go back to the colony right now. Let's go, Fine, Zeke!
van_oasis: Oh, hey! Sorry I never properly introduced myself. I'm Van, and these are Zeke and Fine. They're a bit... peculiar. Fine doesn't remember anything about her past, but did you see what they did with the Shield Liger? We're unstoppable now!

The bandits? They've been prowling around more than usual. A kidnapped girl? I haven't seen anything, but if the bandits are behind it, they must have her at their camp. I've spotted them moving east.
Let me help you, I owe you one for the ruins. Grab your gear from the colony and we'll head out.
van_tied: Agghhh! Father Leon thinks handing over Zeke is the only way to save Maria. But I don't trust those bandits! They're scoundrels, we can't let them get away with this.
I'm sorry, but I need your help again. Father Leon and the patriarch are heading to the exchange point. You need to catch their boss off guard while they're focused on that. It's our only chance.
Don't worry about me, I'll get out of here and take care of getting Zeke back.
watchman_combat_tips: Hey, you're the one who faced that bandit, right? Let me give you some advice about using weapons. As a pilot it might not seem like it, but the more Sleepers you take down, the sharper your combat instincts get. Practice makes perfect, as they say.

By learning about the weapons of a new Zoid species, you also learn better ways to use your own. On top of that, the more you pilot your Zoids, the more used to their weapons you become. Experience counts for a lot out there.
So keep fighting, keep learning, and keep piloting. You'll be a force to be reckoned with in no time.
woman: Thank you for saving me! But please, you have to help me! The bandits... they took my daughter! They dragged her towards Wind Colony!
I'm desperate... she's just a child. Please, you have to save her! Wind Colony is to the north. Be careful, the road is dangerous.
Take this, it might be useful to you. Those bandits were using these devices to command the Zoids around this camp and keep soldiers from getting close.
I managed to snatch one from the bandit who took my daughter. I don't really know what it is, but maybe that old man in the village would know.
woman_reunion: My baby! You brought her back! I can't believe it... I thought I'd never see her again. Thank you... truly, thank you. I'll never forget what you've done for us.
Mom! I missed you so much! I was so scared, but this dude saved me!
Kara! Mind your manners, that's no way to speak about someone who helped us.

Listen... I need to ask you another favor. My husband has been researching Zoids for a long time. He says his work is very important. I don't know the details, but it seems it all started when he met someone called Dr. T.

The bandits were after him... and I don't think they'll stop. So please... take my daughter with you. I know it's a lot to ask, but it's the safest thing for her. If you could bring her to her father...
But Mom! I don't want to leave without you!

My darling, listen to me... I've already asked the Captain to keep your brother safe through his contacts in the army. He'll be working with one of the engineers. And I'll be leaving too, somewhere else... If we split up, it'll be much harder for them to find us.

I promise we'll see each other again. But right now I need you to be brave for me... Please, take care of my daughter. And tell my husband that dangerous people are after him, but that we're safe. Last I heard from him, he was working somewhere near Porto Nido.
arthur_intro: Halt! I am Major Arthur Borgman of the Helic Republic Army. Reports came in about bandits in this area. Normally it's not a Major's job to deal with highway robbers, but I can't call myself a pilot if I turn my back on people who need help.

However, I arrive to find that someone already resolved the whole situation... controlling multiple Zoids at once. That means you possess a Sleeper module. Classified Republican army technology. As a Major, it is my duty to confiscate it.
arthur_post_battle: Ha ha... Not bad, kid! It's true that my duty is to stop you and confiscate that module. The rules are clear. But I've fought enough wars to know that rules aren't always right.
If that technology is in the hands of someone who uses it to protect others, then that's exactly where it should be. Let's pretend this fight never happened.
Although... I have a better proposition. How about joining the Republican Army? If you enlist, that module stops being stolen technology and becomes official equipment. I'd personally recommend you.
arthur_decision: Would you join the Helic Republic Army?
arthur_accept: That's what I like to hear! This is the kind of spirit the Republican army needs. Your first order is to report to the Republican camp. They'll give you your official assignment there. I'll handle the paperwork, you just worry about getting there. Who knows, maybe one day you'll make it into our special unit.
arthur_already_faction: So you've already chosen a side. I won't pretend I'm not disappointed, but a pilot who stands by their convictions has my respect. I hope your commanders are worthy of your loyalty. Whatever happens next, remember: war has a way of testing alliances. Stay sharp out there.
arthur_same_faction: Welcome aboard, soldier. Report to the Republican camp as soon as you can. They're expecting you.
arthur_decline: Hmm... I understand. Not everyone wants to follow orders, and there's nothing wrong with that. But remember: there are forces out there far more dangerous than a few bandits. If you ever change your mind, look me up. The offer stands.
dr_thrun_ruins: Kara!? What are you doing here? How did you get to this place?
You! What are you doing with my daughter? Get away from her right now!
And why would a stranger like you help my daughter? What do you want? Kara, come here. I don't trust him.
Enough, Dad! Listen! He saved us. Mom asked me to come find you. There are dangerous people looking for you... Just listen! ....
I see... Forgive me, Kara. I shouldn't have put you through that. And forgive me too, I don't know what I would have done if something had happened to my family. I owe you a great debt.
So you have one of those Sleeper modules from the bandits... May I take a look?
If you'll allow me... I believe you should tell them about your work. I've seen enough to know this young man can be trusted.
You're right. But not here. These ruins are more dangerous than they seem. Let's head to the city, I'll tell you everything there.
dr_t_find_tortoise: Dr. Thrun? He went back to the Sommerso Ruins — he is convinced there is more to learn about the ancient Core and its connection to the Sleeper system. He will be gone for a while. I am sorry, but I can try to help you...

So you want to attract an Ancient Tortoise? Interesting. Cannon Tortoise are fascinating creatures — highly social, they form tight clusters and the older ones watch over the group from elevated ground. If something were to disturb the younger ones — frighten them enough to flee — the eldest would emerge to guide them to safety. It is a guardian instinct that runs deeper than anything the armies could breed out of them.

You would have to cause enough commotion to scare a group into retreating. I will not pretend it is an elegant approach, but Zoids are resilient — a few honest battles will not cause them harm. It is how they test boundaries in the wild.

Head to Elmia Desert — a group of Cannon Tortoise has been spotted there recently. If your Ancient Tortoise is still out there, it will come out to protect them. But be warned — when it does, it will not be happy. An elder defending its own is not something to take lightly.
dr_t_intro: I'm sorry about what happened, I was only trying to protect myself. They call me Dr. T. Let me guess... this young lady is Dr. Thrun's family, isn't she? The resemblance is unmistakable. Tell me, what brings you here?
...I see. So her family is in danger. I wish I could do more, but what I can do is take this young lady to her father, if her loyal protector will allow me.

There are ancient ruins partially submerged along the shore, not far from here. The locals call them Sommerso. No one dares go near them... but he ventured in without hesitation to continue his research.

The problem is he's become so obsessed with his work that he refuses to come out. I've tried to convince him, but it's no use. If you want to talk to him, you'll have to venture into the ruins and reach him yourselves. I warn you, the path won't be easy.
narration_dr_t_appears: The clash of metal echoes across the port. Merchants scatter, crates topple, and a crowd gathers around the wreckage of the battle.
Then, a voice rises above the commotion. "That's enough! This is a port, not a battlefield." The guard pulls back his Zoid without a word.
Something about that soldier was off. His armor, his uniform... as if he belonged to another time entirely.
concho_accept: Heh. Smart kid. Get yourself to the Imperial recruitment headquarters and enlist. Tell them Concho sent you. Or don't. I don't care either way, they'll figure it out.
concho_already_faction: How pathetic that you chose the losing side. That Sleeper junk was always trash anyway. But hey... next time we meet, it'll be on the battlefield. And I won't go easy on you.
concho_same_faction: Heh, so you're interested in the offer. Then go to the Imperial headquarters and register. Now get lost, I've got better things to do.
concho_decision: The Guylos Empire could use someone who fights with that kind of strength. You in or what?
concho_decline: Tch. Figures. The Empire doesn't beg, kid. Keep your little module, I never gave a damn about that scrap tech anyway. Just don't get in the Empire's way, or I'll crush you myself.
concho_intro: So you're the brat who's been causing trouble around here. Controlling a bunch of Zoids with some gadget. Big deal. I'm Concho Cancer, Guylos Empire. They sent me to check on the mess in this area, but looks like someone already cleaned it up.
Doesn't matter though. You're walking around with military hardware and I'm supposed to care about that. So let's get this over with. Show me what you've got, kid.
concho_post_battle: Hah! Alright, alright. You actually fight hard enough to be entertaining. Most idiots fold in five seconds against my Heldigunner.

Look, I hate speeches, so here's the deal. That module? Honestly, I always thought Sleeper tech was worthless garbage. But you're not garbage. The Guylos Empire wants strong pilots. We don't care about your past, your methods, or your ideals. Just whether you can fight.
dr_thrun_return_to_city: ...and that's how we escaped from those guys in the desert. Their boss said someone hired them to capture Dad in exchange for Sleeper Modules.

...I see. You also mentioned finding a small Zoid sealed in a capsule in those ruins. One that fused with a destroyed Zoid and restored it. That is an Organoid. But this statue... it is nothing like one.
Let me take another look... Hmm. Its structure resembles a Zoid Core, but not like any I have ever seen. It is more like a relic, an ancient Core from a time long before ours.

Even so, that does not explain the pulse. A dormant Core should not be able to shut down a Zoid's instincts like that. I have visited different time periods and even other worlds, yet I have never seen anything like this.

If you would allow me, I would like to study this further. During my time in the Republican Army, I knew someone who dedicated her life to studying ancient Zoid species and their Cores. If anyone can help us unravel this mystery, it is her. She lives on Chimera Island.

I know it is a lot to ask, but could you take us there? We have no transport Zoids, and in times of war the armies have claimed most of them. You will need a Zoid capable of swimming to make the crossing.
dr_thrun_backstory: I think you deserve the full truth. My name is Konrad Thrun. I am a Zoid researcher. Years ago, I developed something called the Sleeper Module, designed to give Zoids a form of autonomy.

But research costs money. I approached both the Helic Republic and the Guylos Empire for funding. At first they were interested in the science, but soon it became a race. An autonomous Zoid means fewer pilots at risk and greater firepower. Each side wanted the technology for their own ends.
What I was creating was not going to become a weapon. It already was one. So I delivered both sides an incomplete version. Enough to be useful, but not enough to be dangerous.
Around that time, I met Dr. T. He is not from this dimension. A Zoid technology malfunction in his world sent him here. When we compared notes, we discovered something unsettling.
In his world, Sleeper technology also exists. But there, they are black boxes that completely replace the pilot. The Zoids obey without question.
Two worlds, the same technology, developed independently. That cannot be a coincidence. It is as if the Zoids themselves were made to adopt this technology.
I knew it was only a matter of time before the armies discovered what the full Sleeper Module could really do. So I disappeared. Brought my research here, to these forgotten ruins.

I know what you are thinking. That this was a bad idea from the start. But for you to understand the true purpose of my research, I need to show you. Let us go back to the ruins, all the way to the bottom. Be prepared, it will be more dangerous this time.
dr_thrun_stray_intro: Did you see that? That Zoid has no Sleeper Module. It barely tried to defend itself, did it? Long ago, Zoids were wild creatures with their own instincts. They hunted, they survived, they lived by their own nature.

Then humans began to domesticate them, to modify them for battle. In return, the Zoids no longer needed to hunt. Humans fed them, kept them in shape, repaired them. But as wars escalated, the armies wanted more efficient weapons.

They stripped away the Zoids' biological needs and replaced them with synthetic fuel and artificial parts. For the Zoids, it was the end of their nature. They became dependent on us and their only purpose became following orders. But this poses a problem...

What happens to Zoids that can no longer fight? Or those that lose their pilots? Or simply the ones that are no longer useful? Exactly what happened to that Helcat. Without their wild instincts, Stray Zoids cannot feed themselves or sustain their own needs. They simply... await their death.
The Sleeper Module was never meant to be a weapon. I designed it to restore what was taken from them. Their autonomy. Their will to survive.

Your Zoids, for example, can follow your commands through instinct thanks to the Sleeper Module. However, it is incomplete. Just a fragment of what the full system can do. Instincts still tethered to a master.
Now come. Deeper in these ruins, there is another Zoid you need to see.
narration_stray_helcat: The Stray Helcat moved with terrifying speed. Its eyes burned with an intensity you had never seen in a Zoid before.
Did you see that? That Helcat has an advanced Sleeper Module. Its instincts are fully restored. It fights like a wild beast, the way Zoids were before humans domesticated them.
The Sleeper Module can restore their autonomy. Make them faster, stronger, more aggressive than any laboratory-bred Zoid. They become true predators once again.

The scarcity of wild Zoids is one of the armies' greatest limitations. If they discover this, they will want this power. Imagine an entire fleet of Zoids with the ferocity of wild beasts but under their command. That is what they will try to achieve.
But these Zoids cannot be controlled. The longer the advanced Sleeper Module runs, the more ferocious they become. They do not obey. They do not stop. They do not recognize friend or foe.
The armies will not care. They will experiment until they find a way to bend these creatures to their will. And when they fail... the destruction will be immeasurable.
D-dad... it... it is getting back up...
No! We need to get out of here, now!
narration_stray_gets_up: The Stray Helcat collapses... but only for a moment. Its limbs twitch, then lock into place. It rises again, more aggressive than before.
It is getting back up! Its Sleeper Module is pushing it beyond its limits. Quick, it will not stop!
coast_duel_challenge: It is a good thing you agreed to come quietly, Miss Corin. Can you imagine the scandal if the army found out one of their officers was beaten on a simple escort mission?
To be fair, the capable pilots I knew in the Republic have all retired by now. I suppose that is why they send this kind of officer these days.
What did you just say?! You think I am some second-rate soldier?! I have commanded more operations than you can count!
Then you would have no problem proving it. A duel. One Zoid against one Zoid. If we win, you leave this island and Miss Corin stays. If you win, she goes with you without resistance.
I was not sent here to play games. My mission is to bring Corin back, not to entertain challenges from strangers.
Of course. I would not expect someone like you to accept. After all, it would be terribly embarrassing if you lost, would it not? Better to hide behind your orders.
...You will regret those words. Fine! I accept! Name the place!
The coast. We settle this in the water.
In the water?! Ha! That is MY element! The finest aquatic units in the Republic are at my command. There is absolutely no way you can win!
This will be safer for the island Zoids, but much harder for us. Prepare your best aquatic Zoid.
narration_ancient_pulse: The Stray Helcat rises once more. Its body trembles with unnatural energy, ready to strike again.
It lunges at you, but before it can reach you, the ancient statue at the center of the ruins begins to glow. A deep pulse radiates outward, like a heartbeat from the stone itself.
The Helcat freezes mid-step. Its eyes dim. Its limbs go slack. It turns and vanishes into the depths of the ruins.
What... what just happened? Did that statue react to the Helcat?
I have no idea. In all my years studying these ruins, I have never seen anything like that. That pulse... it shut down its instincts completely.
We need to leave. It is not safe. We will talk about this in town.
